revision.comment: /* top */ "pound sign" is £, it has no meaning in wikitext. What is meant is #, hash
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Man and his Occupations==== ''Expected quantity:'' 55 {| !A1!!A2!!A3!!A4!!A5!!A6!!A7!!A8!!A9!!A10 |- |A1 |A2 |A3 |A4 |A5 |A6 |A7 |A8 |A9 |A10 |- !A11!!A12!!A13!!A14!!A15!!A16!!A17!!A18!!A19!!A20 |- |A11 |A12 |A13 |A14 |A15 |A16 |A17 |A18 |A19 |A20 |- !A21!!A22!!A23!!A24!!A25!!A26!!A27!!A28!!A29!!A30 |- |A21 |A22 |A23 |A24 |A25 |A26 |A27 |A28 |A29 |A30 |- !A31!!A32!!A33!!A34!!A35!!A36!!A37!!A38!!A39!!A40 |- |A31 |A32 |A33 |A34 |A35 |A36 |A37 |A38 |A39 |A40 |- !A41!!A42!!A43!!A44!!A45!!A46!!A47!!A48!!A49!!A50 |- |A41 |A42 |A43 |A44 |A45 |A46 |A47 |A48 |A49 |A50 |- !A51!!A52!!A53!!A54!!A55!!A56!!A57!!A58!!A59!!A60 |- |A51 |A52 |A53 |A54 |A55 |A56 |A57 |A58 |A59 |A60 |} ====B. Agriculture, Crafts, and Professions==== ''Expected quantity:'' {| !U1!!U2!!U3!!U4!!U5!!U6!!U7!!U8!!U9!!U10 |- |U1 |U2 |U3 |U4 |U5 |U6 |U7 |U8 |U9 |U10 |- !U11!!U12!!U13!!U14!!U15!!U16!!U17!!U18!!U19!!U20 |- |U11 |U12 |U13 |U14 |U15 |U16 |U17 |U18 |U19 |U20 |- !U21!!U22!!U23!!U24!!U25!!U26!!U27!!U28!!U29!!U30 |- |U21 |U22 |U23 |U24 |U25 |U26 |U27 |U28 |U29 |U30 |- !U31!!U32!!U33!!U34!!U35!!U36!!U37!!U38!!U39!!U40 |- |U31 |U32 |U33 |U34 |U35 |U36 |U37 |U38 |U39 |U40 |- !U41!!U42!!U43!!U44!!U45!!U46!!U47!!U48!!U49!!U50 |- |U41 |U42 |U43 |U44 |U45 |U46 |U47 |U48 |U49 |U50 |} ====V. Rope, Fiber, Baskets, Bags, etc.==== ''Expected quantity:'' 38 {| !V1!!V2!!V3!!V4!!V5!!V6!!V7!!V8!!V9!!V10 |- |V1 |V2 |V3 |V4 |V5 |V6 |V7 |V8 |V9 |V10 |- !V11!!V12!!V13!!V14!!V15!!V16!!V17!!V18!!V19!!V20 |- |V11 |V12 |V13 |V14 |V15 |V16 |V17 |V18 |V19 |V20 |- !V21!!V22!!V23!!V24!!V25!!V26!!V27!!V28!!V29!!V30 |- |V21 |V22 |V23 |V24 |V25 |V26 |V27 |V28 |V29 |V30 |- !V31!!V32!!V33!!V34!!V35!!V36!!V37!!V38!!V39!!V40 |- |V31 |V32 |V33 |V34 |V35 |V36 |V37 |V38 |V39 |V40 |} ====W. Vessels of Stone and Earthenware==== ''Expected quantity:'' 25 {| !W1!!W2!!W3!!W4!!W5!!W6!!W7!!W8!!W9!!W10 |- |W1 |W2 |W3 |W4 |W5 |W6 |W7 |W8 |W9 |W10 |- !W11!!W12!!W13!!W14!!W15!!W16!!W17!!W18!!W19!!W20 |- |W11 |W12 |W13 |W14 |W15 |W16 |W17 |W18 |W19 |W20 |- !W21!!W22!!W23!!W24!!W25!!W26!!W27!!W28!!W29!!W30 |- |W21 |W22 |W23 |W24 |W25 |W26 |W27 |W28 |W29 |W30 |} ====X. Loaves and Cakes==== ''Expected quantity:'' 8 {| !X1!!X2!!X3!!X4!!X5!!X6!!X7!!X8!!X9!!X10 |- |X1 |X2 |X3 |X4 |X5 |X6 |X7 |X8 |X9 |X10 |} ====Y. Writings, Games, Music==== ''Expected quantity:'' 8 {| !Y1!!Y2!!Y3!!Y4!!Y5!!Y6!!Y7!!Y8!!Y9!!Y10 |- |Y1 |Y2 |Y3 |Y4 |Y5 |Y6 |Y7 |Y8 |Y9 |Y10 |} ====Z. Strokes, Signs derived from Hieratic, Geometrical Figures==== ''Expected quantity:'' 11 {| !Z1!!Z2!!Z3!!Z4!!Z5!!Z6!!Z7!!Z8!!Z9!!Z10 |- |Z1 |Z2 |Z3 |Z4 |Z5 |Z6 |Z7 |Z8 |Z9 |Z10 |- !Z11!!Z12!!Z13!!Z14!!Z15!!Z16!!Z17!!Z18!!Z19!!Z20 |- |Z11 |Z12 |Z13 |Z14 |Z15 |Z16 |Z17 |Z18 |Z19 |Z20 |} ====Aa. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: mass importing wikipedia pages
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents Rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Rope, Fiber, Baskets, Bags, etc.==== ''Expected quantity:'' 38 {| !V1!!V2!!V3!!V4!!V5!!V6!!V7!!V8!!V9!!V10 |- |V1 |V2 |V3 |V4 |V5 |V6 |V7 |V8 |V9 |V10 |- !V11!!V12!!V13!!V14!!V15!!V16!!V17!!V18!!V19!!V20 |- |V11 |V12 |V13 |V14 |V15 |V16 |V17 |V18 |V19 |V20 |- !V21!!V22!!V23!!V24!!V25!!V26!!V27!!V28!!V29!!V30 |- |V21 |V22 |V23 |V24 |V25 |V26 |V27 |V28 |V29 |V30 |- !V31!!V32!!V33!!V34!!V35!!V36!!V37!!V38!!V39!!V40 |- |V31 |V32 |V33 |V34 |V35 |V36 |V37 |V38 |V39 |V40 |} ====W. Strokes, Signs derived from Hieratic, Geometrical Figures==== ''Expected quantity:'' 11 {| !Z1!!Z2!!Z3!!Z4!!Z5!!Z6!!Z7!!Z8!!Z9!!Z10 |- |Z1 |Z2 |Z3 |Z4 |Z5 |Z6 |Z7 |Z8 |Z9 |Z10 |- !Z11!!Z12!!Z13!!Z14!!Z15!!Z16!!Z17!!Z18!!Z19!!Z20 |- |Z11 |Z12 |Z13 |Z14 |Z15 |Z16 |Z17 |Z18 |Z19 |Z20 |} ====Aa. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents Rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Rope, Fiber, Baskets, Bags, etc.==== ''Expected quantity:'' 38 {| !V1!!V2!!V3!!V4!!V5!!V6!!V7!!V8!!V9!!V10 |- |V1 |V2 |V3 |V4 |V5 |V6 |V7 |V8 |V9 |V10 |- !V11!!V12!!V13!!V14!!V15!!V16!!V17!!V18!!V19!!V20 |- |V11 |V12 |V13 |V14 |V15 |V16 |V17 |V18 |V19 |V20 |- !V21!!V22!!V23!!V24!!V25!!V26!!V27!!V28!!V29!!V30 |- |V21 |V22 |V23 |V24 |V25 |V26 |V27 |V28 |V29 |V30 |- !V31!!V32!!V33!!V34!!V35!!V36!!V37!!V38!!V39!!V40 |- |V31 |V32 |V33 |V34 |V35 |V36 |V37 |V38 |V39 |V40 |} ====W. Strokes, Signs derived from Hieratic, Geometrical Figures==== ''Expected quantity:'' 11 {| !Z1!!Z2!!Z3!!Z4!!Z5!!Z6!!Z7!!Z8!!Z9!!Z10 |- |Z1 |Z2 |Z3 |Z4 |Z5 |Z6 |Z7 |Z8 |Z9 |Z10 |- !Z11!!Z12!!Z13!!Z14!!Z15!!Z16!!Z17!!Z18!!Z19!!Z20 |- |Z11 |Z12 |Z13 |Z14 |Z15 |Z16 |Z17 |Z18 |Z19 |Z20 |} ====Aa. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents Rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Rope, Fiber, Baskets, Bags, etc.==== ''Expected quantity:'' 38 {| !V1!!V2!!V3!!V4!!V5!!V6!!V7!!V8!!V9!!V10 |- |V1 |V2 |V3 |V4 |V5 |V6 |V7 |V8 |V9 |V10 |- !V11!!V12!!V13!!V14!!V15!!V16!!V17!!V18!!V19!!V20 |- |V11 |V12 |V13 |V14 |V15 |V16 |V17 |V18 |V19 |V20 |- !V21!!V22!!V23!!V24!!V25!!V26!!V27!!V28!!V29!!V30 |- |V21 |V22 |V23 |V24 |V25 |V26 |V27 |V28 |V29 |V30 |- !V31!!V32!!V33!!V34!!V35!!V36!!V37!!V38!!V39!!V40 |- |V31 |V32 |V33 |V34 |V35 |V36 |V37 |V38 |V39 |V40 |} ====W. Strokes, Signs derived from Hieratic, Geometrical Figures==== ''Expected quantity:'' 11 {| !Z1!!Z2!!Z3!!Z4!!Z5!!Z6!!Z7!!Z8!!Z9!!Z10 |- |Z1 |Z2 |Z3 |Z4 |Z5 |Z6 |Z7 |Z8 |Z9 |Z10 |- !Z11!!Z12!!Z13!!Z14!!Z15!!Z16!!Z17!!Z18!!Z19!!Z20 |- |Z11 |Z12 |Z13 |Z14 |Z15 |Z16 |Z17 |Z18 |Z19 |Z20 |} ====Aa. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents Rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Rope, Fiber, Baskets, Bags, etc.==== ''Expected quantity:'' 38 {| !V1!!V2!!V3!!V4!!V5!!V6!!V7!!V8!!V9!!V10 |- |V1 |V2 |V3 |V4 |V5 |V6 |V7 |V8 |V9 |V10 |- !V11!!V12!!V13!!V14!!V15!!V16!!V17!!V18!!V19!!V20 |- |V11 |V12 |V13 |V14 |V15 |V16 |V17 |V18 |V19 |V20 |- !V21!!V22!!V23!!V24!!V25!!V26!!V27!!V28!!V29!!V30 |- |V21 |V22 |V23 |V24 |V25 |V26 |V27 |V28 |V29 |V30 |- !V31!!V32!!V33!!V34!!V35!!V36!!V37!!V38!!V39!!V40 |- |V31 |V32 |V33 |V34 |V35 |V36 |V37 |V38 |V39 |V40 |} ====W. Strokes, Signs derived from Hieratic, Geometrical Figures==== ''Expected quantity:'' 11 {| !Z1!!Z2!!Z3!!Z4!!Z5!!Z6!!Z7!!Z8!!Z9!!Z10 |- |Z1 |Z2 |Z3 |Z4 |Z5 |Z6 |Z7 |Z8 |Z9 |Z10 |- !Z11!!Z12!!Z13!!Z14!!Z15!!Z16!!Z17!!Z18!!Z19!!Z20 |- |Z11 |Z12 |Z13 |Z14 |Z15 |Z16 |Z17 |Z18 |Z19 |Z20 |} ====Aa. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[:en:Ox|Ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[:en:Ox|Ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents Rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Rope, Fiber, Baskets, Bags, etc.==== ''Expected quantity:'' 38 {| !V1!!V2!!V3!!V4!!V5!!V6!!V7!!V8!!V9!!V10 |- |V1 |V2 |V3 |V4 |V5 |V6 |V7 |V8 |V9 |V10 |- !V11!!V12!!V13!!V14!!V15!!V16!!V17!!V18!!V19!!V20 |- |V11 |V12 |V13 |V14 |V15 |V16 |V17 |V18 |V19 |V20 |- !V21!!V22!!V23!!V24!!V25!!V26!!V27!!V28!!V29!!V30 |- |V21 |V22 |V23 |V24 |V25 |V26 |V27 |V28 |V29 |V30 |- !V31!!V32!!V33!!V34!!V35!!V36!!V37!!V38!!V39!!V40 |- |V31 |V32 |V33 |V34 |V35 |V36 |V37 |V38 |V39 |V40 |} ====W. Strokes, Signs derived from Hieratic, Geometrical Figures==== ''Expected quantity:'' 11 {| !Z1!!Z2!!Z3!!Z4!!Z5!!Z6!!Z7!!Z8!!Z9!!Z10 |- |Z1 |Z2 |Z3 |Z4 |Z5 |Z6 |Z7 |Z8 |Z9 |Z10 |- !Z11!!Z12!!Z13!!Z14!!Z15!!Z16!!Z17!!Z18!!Z19!!Z20 |- |Z11 |Z12 |Z13 |Z14 |Z15 |Z16 |Z17 |Z18 |Z19 |Z20 |} ====Aa. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
"WikiHiero" is a PHP script to render [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]] as PNG images. Since Egyptian hieroglyphs are currently only partially supported by [[:en:Unicode|Unicode]], this is the only way of rendering hieroglyphs in article texts short of uploading custom images. The script is invoked by the tag. The tag produces an HTML table (of class='mw-hierotable') that will separate the surrounding text into paragraphs. For inline WikiHiero images, manually add
tags around the paragraph and tags around the hieroglyphs. For example, the code: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[:en:Ox|Ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
 produces: :
[[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] is thought to be derived from the West Semitic word for "[[:en:Ox|Ox]]", and its shape is ultimately based on a [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|hieroglyph]] depicting an ox's head, F1, in Egyptian reading a [[:en:Egyptian biliteral signs|biliteral sign]] with the phonetic value {{lang|egy-Latn|ı͗ḥ}}.
(If the paragraph is set off with a colon, hash or asterisk, the
tags are not necessary.) Template {{tl|hiero}} is intended for [[Wikipedia:Boxes|boxes]] containing hieroglyphic script. See [[:mw:Extension:WikiHiero]] for the project page on [[:mw:|MediaWiki]]. == Hieroglyphs == There are two ways of encoding hieroglyphs: *using Gardiner's sign list code (e.g. A12) *with the more frequent phonemes (e.g. xA) === Gardiner's sign list === {{see|Gardiner's sign list}} A hieroglyph is encoded by: *a letter that represents the category *a number that represents Rank within the category ''NB until the appropriate software is enabled, the encoding will be displayed instead of the hieroglyph.'' ====A. Unclassified==== ''Expected quantity:'' 31 {| !Aa1!!Aa2!!Aa3!!Aa4!!Aa5!!Aa6!!Aa7!!Aa8!!Aa9!!Aa10 |- |Aa1 |Aa2 |Aa3 |Aa4 |Aa5 |Aa6 |Aa7 |Aa8 |Aa9 |Aa10 |- !Aa11!!Aa12!!Aa13!!Aa14!!Aa15!!Aa16!!Aa17!!Aa18!!Aa19!!Aa20 |- |Aa11 |Aa12 |Aa13 |Aa14 |Aa15 |Aa16 |Aa17 |Aa18 |Aa19 |Aa20 |- !Aa21!!Aa22!!Aa23!!Aa24!!Aa25!!Aa26!!Aa27!!Aa28!!Aa29!!Aa30 |- |Aa21 |Aa22 |Aa23 |Aa24 |Aa25 |Aa26 |Aa27 |Aa28 |Aa29 |Aa30 |- !Aa31!!Aa32!!Aa33!!Aa34!!Aa35!!Aa36!!Aa37!!Aa38!!Aa39!!Aa40 |- |Aa31 |Aa32 |Aa33 |Aa34 |Aa35 |Aa36 |Aa37 |Aa38 |Aa39 |Aa40 |- !Aa41!!Aa42!!Aa43!!Aa44!!Aa45!!Aa46!!Aa47!!Aa48!!Aa49!!Aa50 |- |Aa41 |Aa42 |Aa43 |Aa44 |Aa45 |Aa46 |Aa47 |Aa48 |Aa49 |Aa50 |} === Phonemes === {{See also|mw:Extension:WikiHiero/phonemes|Transliteration of Ancient Egyptian}} == Codes == Codes are characters that: * Separate hieroglyphs * Indicate the organization of the hieroglyphs in space (that is, the position of hieroglyphs in a block) === Separator === The official hieroglyph separator is the hyphen character ("-", also called ''block separator'') but you are also allowed to use a space because it is a widespread practice. ====Example==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1 |height="40px"|A1-B1 |- !allowed |A1 B1 |A1 B1 |- !incorrect |A1B1 |height="40px"|A1B1 |} === Superposition === To superpose hieroglyphs, use the colon character (":") instead of the hyphen. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px" align="center"|p-t |- !superposed |p:t |height="40px" align="center"|p:t |} === Juxtaposition === To juxtapose hieroglyphs, use the asterisk character ("*"). Juxtaposing hieroglyphs is only relevant when they are part of a block which is superposed. ====Example==== {| border="1" !normal layout |p-t |height="40px"|p-t |- !juxtaposed
(same as normal layout) |p*t |height="40px"|p*t |- !rowspan="2"|correct |p*t:a |height="40px"|p*t:a |- |p*p*p:t*t*t |height="40px"|p*p*p:t*t*t |} === End of line === To make a new line of text, use the exclamation character ("!"). Normally, the "end-of-line" must be surrounded by separators (as -!-), but space or nothing are also allowed. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !correct |A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-!-C1-D1 |- !allowed |A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1 ! C1-D1 |- !allowed
''although apparently
''not'' fully functional'' |A1-B1! C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1! C1-D1 |} === Orientation === Asymmetrical characters may be reversed by appending the virgule /. This does not change the direction of the text. A few characters may be rotated by appending 'V' for 'vertical'. A character may be raised or lowered by pairing it with H_SPACE. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |A1-B1-C1-D1 |height="80px"|A1-B1-C1-D1 |- !Reversed orientation |A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |height="80px"|A1\-B1\-C1\-D1\ |- !Horizontal (default) |Y1 |height="40px"|Y1 |- !Rotated (vertical) |Y1V |height="40px"|Y1V |- !Inline |G47-Z1 |height="40px"|G47-Z1 |- !Raised |G47-Z1:H_SPACE |height="40px"|G47-Z1:H_SPACE |} Note that the Gardiner number for the rotated form, "Y1a", is not supported. === Cartouche === A cartouche is created with <-...->. ====Examples==== {| border="1" !Default |<-pr\:aA\-> |height="80px"|<-pr\:aA\-> |} ==See also== *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Ancient Near East]] *[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Writing systems]] {{Wikipedia technical help|collapsed}} [[Category:MediaWiki Help]]
